name: Valhalla Routing Engine
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); self-hosted. Stadia Maps / Mapbox offer hosted Valhalla with per-request pricing.

auth.notes: Self-hosted: no auth. Stadia Maps hosted: API key required.

performance.notes: Sub-20ms for typical routes; isochrone computation 50-200ms; slightly slower than OSRM but more features

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source routing engine with tiled data model (incremental map updates without full reprocessing). Services: route, matrix, isochrone, map matching, optimized route (TSP), elevation, transit. Supports multiple cost models (time, distance, multimodal). Better isochrone support than OSRM. Python/JS bindings available. Used by Mapbox, Stadia Maps, and major logistics companies.

gotchas: Tiled data model requires more complex setup than OSRM but enables incremental updates
JSON request format verbose — costing models have many parameters
Self-hosting requires significant memory for global tiles (~100GB for planet)
Less documentation examples than OSRM for edge cases
best_when: Isochrone generation (reachable area within N minutes)
Self-hosted routing needing incremental OSM map updates
Logistics optimization with time windows and multiple costing profiles
avoid_when: Simple A-to-B routing only (OSRM faster and simpler)
Real-time traffic routing (static profiles only for self-hosted)
Teams without infrastructure to host and maintain routing tiles
summary: Valhalla is an open-source routing engine with a tiled data model enabling incremental map updates. Provides routing, isochrone generation, matrix, map matching, and multimodal transit services. MIT licensed, self-hostable. Better isochrone and multimodal support than OSRM. Used by Mapbox and logistics companies. Best for applications needing isochrones or multimodal routing.
